bibliography:
Bolton, J. (Ed.). (2019). Data Warehousing Essentials. Larsen and Keller Education.
	
	
	Deckler, G. (2022). Learn Power BI: A comprehensive, step-by-step guide for beginners to learn real-world business intelligence, 2nd Edition (2nd ed. edition). Packt Publishing
	
	
	Inmon, B., & Puppini, F. (2020). The Unified Star Schema: An Agile and Resilient Approach to Data Warehouse and Analytics Design. Technics Publications.
	
	
	Kelly, N. (2021). Delivering Data Analytics: A Step-By-Step Guide to Driving Adoption of Business Intelligence from Planning to Launch (1st edition). Kogan Page.
	
	
	Kimball, R. (2008). The Data Warehouse Lifecycle Toolkit, 2nd Edition (2 edition). Wiley.
	
	
	Kimball, R. (2013). The Data Warehouse Toolkit: The Definitive Guide to Dimensional Modeling, 3rd Edition (3 edition). Wiley.
objectives:
Understand why companies need a BI environment
	What architecture components exists for BI
	Modelling Schemas for DW
	Know the several Data Manipulation Processes within a DW Environment
	What can a User get in a Reporting Interface
	Understand how to use LLMs and AI Agents in a decision support systems context
organicUnit: ECATI - School of Communication, Architecture, Arts and Information Technologies
programme:
DW Business Challenges
	DW Architectures
	Modeling the Data Warehouse ¿ Best Practices
	ETL Overview
	BI Databases
	Reporting Tools
	Build Reports
	Business Intelligence Projects with Large Language Models (LLMs) and AI Agents
presentation: The Decision Support Systems / Business Intelligence UC aims to ensure that there is an introduction to the theme of data analysis processes to support the areas of business management. In this sense, UC has a fundamental role in the study cycle in understanding the management and decision processes of organizations, as well as how to put data infrastructures at the service of the corporate business.
avaliacao:
Componente Teórica (Frequência)

  • Participação, Mini Testes, Teste final - 50%
    • Teste Final Frequência (60%)
    • 2 Mini-testes (30%)
    • Participação (10%)

Componente Prática (Frequência)

  • Projetos - 50%
    • 1st Work - Modeling
    • 2nd Work - ETL
    • 3rd Work - Reporting & AI Agents
    • Participation
    • Teams of 3-4 students

Nota mínima em cada componente 9,5 valores em 20.

-------------------------------

Recurso

  • Teste de Recurso (50%)
  • Projeto Final - 50%

Nota mínima em cada componente 9,5 valores em 20.
